$comment: Windy Word (windy-pro) Gen-1 retrofit — FIRST PASS. Every tool here is a REAL, live route on the :18765 control surface, bound via `transport` (the Gen-1 escape hatch) so the hardened main.js is never rewritten. This pass covers the implemented 13-knob baseline + the proven sound/settings tools Windy Fly already drives. The 8 baseline GAPS this retrofit exposed (get_logs, run_selftest, get_capabilities, reconnect, enter/exit_safe_mode, apply_update, reset_to_defaults) are NOT advertised here — an MCP packet must never offer a knob that 404s mid-incident. They live in docs/handoffs/WORD-LANE-2026-07-13.md as the punch list, matching ADR-060 §7. Full enumeration of Word's other ~90 feature routes is a documented follow pass; this manifest proves the retrofit pattern first.

tools.name: get_health
tools.description: The FIRST tool to call when the user says Windy Word is acting up. Runs the built-in doctor and returns a full diagnostic snapshot — what's healthy, what's broken, and a plain-English read on each finding so you can tell the user what's wrong.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Read what Windy Word is doing right now: whether it's recording, idle, or busy, and its current runtime state. Use this before changing anything so you know the starting point.

tools.name: get_config
tools.description: Read Windy Word's full current configuration (secrets omitted). Use this to see how the app is set up before you diagnose a problem or change a setting.

tools.name: check_for_update
tools.description: Ask whether a newer version of Windy Word is available to install. Read-only — this only checks, it does not download or apply anything.

tools.name: restart_app
tools.description: Restart the whole Windy Word app — the standard fix when it's wedged and reconnecting or safe mode won't cut it. Confirm with the user first; unsaved in-flight recording is lost.

tools.name: list_settings
tools.description: List every setting Windy Word exposes, each with its type, current value, allowed values, and a plain description. Read this before set_setting so you change the right knob to a valid value.

tools.name: set_setting
tools.description: Change one Windy Word setting by its path (e.g. the theme, a hotkey, the transcription model). Validated against the catalog and applied live, and every change is recorded so it can be undone. Confirm with the user before changing a setting.

path.description: The setting path from list_settings, e.g. appearance.theme.

value.description: The new value; must match the setting's type and allowed values.

tools.name: undo_last_change
tools.description: Undo the most recent setting change, stepping back through Windy Word's change history. Use this the moment a setting you changed made things worse — a firefighter that can't un-break what it just tried is dangerous.

tools.name: get_sound_state
tools.description: Read the current state of Windy Word's sound effects: which sound pack is active, the master volume, and whether each per-stage sound is on. Use this before turning sounds up, down, or off.

tools.name: list_sound_packs
tools.description: List the sound packs the user can switch Windy Word to, each with its id and friendly name. Read this before set_sound_pack so you pick a pack that actually exists.

tools.name: set_master_volume
tools.description: Set how loud Windy Word's sound effects are overall, from 0 (silent) to 100 (full). Use this when the user says the app is too loud or too quiet.

tools.name: set_sound
tools.description: Turn one of Windy Word's per-stage sounds on or off, or set its volume — the sounds it makes when recording starts, stops, while processing, on a warning, or when it pastes.

enabled.description: Turn this sound on or off.

tools.name: set_sound_pack
tools.description: Switch Windy Word to a different sound pack by its id or friendly name (from list_sound_packs) — for example a quieter pack, or the silent one to turn all effects off.

packId.description: The pack id (or friendly name) from list_sound_packs.

tools.name: get_logs
tools.description: Read Windy Word's recent app logs when something's misbehaving — server starts, recording/paste/engine events, errors — as a timeline of what happened. Content-free by design: each entry is just a timestamp, level, component, and event name; transcripts, clip audio, and settings values are never included.

limit.description: How many recent entries to return (newest last); default 200, max 500.

entries.description: [{ts, level, component, event}] — no content/data payload

tools.name: get_capabilities
tools.description: Probe which Windy Word features actually work on this machine right now — recording, paste, text-to-speech, translation, and voice cloning — each reported as supported, unsupported, or unknown (probed, never assumed). Use this when a user says a feature 'isn't working' to see whether it's even available before digging deeper. Note: this is about FEATURES; /install/capabilities is about installable system dependencies.

tools.name: run_selftest
tools.description: Actively test whether Windy Word's core transcription actually works right now: it transcribes a small bundled audio clip through the real engine and checks that text comes back. Reports pass/fail per stage (engine running, test clip present, transcription produced text) so you can tell whether 'nothing happens when I speak' is the engine being down versus something else. Reports only the transcript's length, never its text.

passed.description: true only when every stage passed ('passed', not 'ok' — top-level ok is reserved for the invoke envelope)

stages.description: [{name: engine|clip|transcribe, ok, detail}] — never the transcript text
